original_abstract:
The concept of the Metaverse has gained significant traction following recent advancements in Web 3.0, blockchain technology, and substantial investments by global technology conglomerates. Despite its growing prominence, the scientific community lacks a unified, contemporary definition of what the Metaverse constitutes. This paper addresses this gap by conducting a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) to collect, analyze, and synthesize existing scientific definitions and their core characteristics. 

The study identifies key recurring attributes and proposes two revised definitions. The first is a streamlined, holistic description of the Metaverse as a three-dimensional online environment where users, represented by avatars, interact within virtual spaces decoupled from the physical world. The second is a more granular definition that integrates technical, social, and economic dimensions, such as persistence, immersion, and decentralization. By identifying open research challenges and exploring the social, technical, and economic implications of this emerging paradigm, this paper provides a conceptual framework for scholars in information systems, computer science, and business informatics. Finally, the authors discuss the necessary preconditions and infrastructure requirements for the successful implementation of a global Metaverse.
humanized_abstract:
Over the last few months the term Metaverse has been constantly appearing in the media and is being discussed among engineers and programmers, most likely due to the evolution of Web 3.0 and blockchain technology as well as large investments announced by global technology companies. Unfortunately, a clear and actual scientific definition of Metaverse does not exist. This work presents a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) that aims to compile, organize and evaluate the various scientific definitions and features found in the literature regarding Metaverse.

This study elaborates on the attributes that appear in the scientific literature on the Metaverse and provides two possible, newly worded definitions of the concept. Definition 1 is a concise, holistic definition of the Metaverse as a 3D online space where users represented by avatars can interact with each other in virtually created spaces that do not correspond to real locations in the physical world. Definition 2 is a more detailed, multi-perspective definition that considers the technological, social and economic aspects of the Metaverse (such as persistence, immersion and decentralization). The study provides a starting point for the resolution of numerous open research questions that arise when one considers the social, technological and economic implications of the Metaverse. Finally, the study discusses prerequisites and technological infrastructure that must be fulfilled for a global Metaverse to exist.
